name: Homemade Maggot Stew
recipeIngredient: 1 pound Stew beef, cut into one-inch chunks
3 medium to 4 Carrots, peeled and cut into small coins
1 cup Fresh or frozen Green beans
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the vegetable oil in a stew pot over medium-low heat (with adult assistance).
2. In a Ziploc bag, mix together flour, salt, pepper, and garlic powder.
3. Add the beef chunks to the bag; seal and shake until the meat is well coated with the seasoning mixture.
4. Pour the seasoned beef into the stew pot.
5. Increase the heat to medium (with adult assistance) and use a long-handled spoon to turn the beef every 3-4 minutes, ensuring it browns evenly on all sides. Continue cooking until the meat develops a crusty exterior.
6. Add the tomatoes, broth, thyme, and bay leaf to the pot. Return the heat to medium and bring the mixture to a boil.
7. Reduce the heat to low and simmer for one hour.
8. Peel and cut the carrots into small coins (with adult assistance).
9. After an additional 45 minutes of simmering, add the carrots and green beans to the pot. Cover and continue simmering for another 45 minutes.
10. Cook the orzo pasta in a saucepan according to package instructions until just tender. Drain through a colander, removing excess water (with adult assistance).
11. Add the cooked orzo to the stew pot; turn off the heat.
Serving Suggestion:
Serve the maggot stew with roasted, baked, or boiled meat and enjoy the creepy-crawly feast!
